description: PA-001: Pool.try_acquire bench harness INCOMPLETE on G104/G108. pytest-benchmark per-call timing model cannot instrument sync-fast-path-in-async shape; profiler yields no allocs/op verdict. Gate left in INCOMPLETE state, not PASS.

PA-002: Pool.aclose bench harness INCOMPLETE on G104/G108. Bulk-teardown shape does not fit per-call harness model; aclose drains all resources in one call, making per-iteration timing meaningless. No allocs/op verdict rendered.

PA-003: perf-budget.md hot_paths declares stub frame IDs that py-spy cannot resolve to real call frames. G109 profile-shape check cannot verify top-10 CPU samples match declared hot paths; gate produces INCOMPLETE-by-design-mismatch verdict.

PA-004: ruff 0.4.10 cannot parse PEP 639 license-files field; dev-extras pin ruff>=0.4,<0.5 predates PEP 639 support. G43-py fires on config-file parsing, not code. python.json toolchain.lint.min_version not enforced. 26 ruff findings unreviewed including ASYNC109 vs TPRD §10 timeout contract.

PA-005: scripts/run-guardrails.sh missing realpath on ACTIVE_PACKAGES_JSON before child guardrails cd into TARGET directory. Path resolution breaks when guardrail cds away from pipeline root. One-line fix: ACTIVE_PACKAGES_JSON=$(realpath "$ACTIVE_PACKAGES_JSON").

PA-006: CLAUDE.md rules 20, 24, 28, 32 reference Go-specific agent names (sdk-perf-architect-go, sdk-benchmark-devil-go, sdk-profile-auditor-go etc.) in language-neutral rule prose. Generalization debt: every Python agent encountering these rules reads Go-flavored terminology. Requires human PR to replace with <lang>-parameterized notation.

PA-007 / SKD-001: conftest.py asyncio_task_tracker fixture not autouse=True. Only 3/62 tests (leak suite) have the asyncio leak guard active. The 59 unit+property tests run unguarded; a task leak introduced in the unit suite would not be caught at CI time. Violates python-asyncio-leak-prevention SKILL.md §Test-gates Rule 1.

PA-008: pip-audit + editable install corner case — pip-audit may skip editable-install dependencies in certain pip versions, producing a false-clean supply chain report. Pattern identified at T-SUPPLY wave; no CVE missed in this run but the audit coverage gap is structural.

PA-009: pytest 8.4.2 carries CVE-2025-71176. G32-py supply-chain scan is INCOMPLETE because safety scan command migration (PA-010) is pending. The CVE may affect test isolation; severity HIGH because test framework is the trust boundary for all 62 test verdicts. No exploit confirmed in resourcepool test suite, but the framework is untrusted at present.

PA-010: safety CLI migrated scan subcommand; G32-py script calls deprecated safety check command. Scan produces exit-code 0 on incompatible CLI version, silently passing the supply-chain gate without scanning. Structural false-negative risk on every future run until script is updated.

PA-011: soak driver for Python T1-tier soak runs exists only as bespoke impl-run artifact; no reusable soak-driver skill exists in the python pack. Pattern needs promotion to python-soak-driver skill so future runs do not re-author the driver from scratch.

PA-012 / SAMPLER-CARRY: asyncio cooperative-yield starvation under hot worker loops causes soak sampler to under-sample during high-throughput soak phases. Structurally identical to Go-pack goroutine-scheduler-starving-soak-sampler pattern. Python soak skill carries no language-neutral sampler-starvation warning. Soak metrics during run-1 archive were unreliable; run-3 soak verdicts trusted but sampler correctness is not formally verified.

PA-013: perf-budget.md oracle values for PoolConfig.__init__ and AcquiredResource.__aenter__ were set using Go×10 margin without floor-bound idiom. Both symbols hit the Python language floor (frozen dataclass construction + context-manager entry are dominated by interpreter overhead). Oracle margin mechanically unreachable; G108 issued CALIBRATION-WARN accepted at H8. No re-calibration was performed before H9.

PA-014: scripts/compute-shape-hash.sh not authored for Python pack. baselines/python/output-shape-history.jsonl seeded manually by metrics-collector without a verified hash. Shape-hash baseline integrity is unverified for this run; ⚠ shape-churn detection will not fire correctly until the script is authored and run.

SKD-001: asyncio_task_tracker fixture in conftest.py not declared autouse=True. Violates python-asyncio-leak-prevention skill Rule 1. 59 of 62 tests run with no task-leak guard; a background task leak introduced in unit tests would not be detected by CI. The 3 leak-suite tests passing does not validate unit-suite absence of leaks.

SKD-002: _pool.py uses except BaseException 4× (L247, L381, L451, L536). Violates python-exception-patterns SKILL.md Rule 4. CancelledError is handled in a preceding arm in all 4 locations mitigating current correctness risk, but the pattern is fragile: dropping the CancelledError arm in a future edit silently swallows cancellation. Refactor to except Exception is safe and closes the rule violation.

SKD-003: pyproject.toml addopts does not include --doctest-modules. All public symbols carry Examples blocks per python-doctest-patterns skill, but examples are never executed by pytest. Skill guarantee (the example cannot lie) is forfeited; docstring drift will not be caught by CI. Fix: add --doctest-modules to addopts (or a dedicated tests/test_doctests.py).

COV-001-DEFERRED-INFO: design-phase convention-devil severity bucket lacks a runtime-impact-unknown class. CV-001 (missing typing import) was classified LOW at design phase; only mypy-strict dynamic verification confirmed it as a runtime-breaking correctness bug. The gap means type-annotation-import defects that break runtime behavior can silently pass design-phase review at LOW severity.

SAMPLER-CARRY (process): the soak-runner skill (shared-core + python pack) carries no language-neutral sampler-starvation warning. The asyncio cooperative-yield starvation pattern (DEF-012) reproduced from Go-pack without any cross-language advisory. Future packs will rediscover independently unless the skill is updated with a cross-language warning section.
